summary: Decompiled two Super Scope item functions, it_80291F14 and it_80291FA8, and tightened related prototypes/attribute layout so charge-level ammo costs can be read from itSScopeAttributes::xC. The PR reports 100% objdiff matches and main.dol SHA1 OK.

key_files.role: Replaced placeholders for it_80291F14 and it_80291FA8 with matched C implementations and added itsscopebeam.h include.
Updated function prototypes for it_80291D38, it_80291F14, and it_80291FA8 from UNK signatures to typed Item_GObj/Vec3/int/float signatures.
Split itSScopeAttributes padding at +0xC into float xC[10], preserving total size before Vec3 x34 at +0x34.

what_changed: itSScopeAttributes changed from /* +C */ char pad_xC[0x28] to /* +C */ float xC[10], exposing a ten-entry float table used by Super Scope charge levels.

it_80291F14(Item_GObj* gobj, int charge_level) now gets the Item and attributes, chooses an ammo/charge cost from attrs->xC[charge_level] for cases 0 through 9, subtracts it from ip->xD4C, and clamps ip->xD4C to zero.

it_80291FA8(Item_GObj* gobj, Vec3* pos, int charge_level, float scale) now computes cost through it_80291D38(gobj, charge_level), subtracts/clamps ip->xD4C, and calls it_80298DEC(ip->owner, pos, charge_level, scale).
Included itsscopebeam.h in itsscope.c, providing the declaration needed for the beam-related call it_80298DEC.

Header declarations were refined: it_80291D38 returns int and takes Item_GObj*, int; it_80291F14 returns void and takes Item_GObj*, int; it_80291FA8 returns void and takes Item_GObj*, Vec3*, int, float.
smart_moves: Used the attribute layout to replace anonymous padding with float xC[10], making the charge-level table visible while preserving offsets through x34.
Kept charge/ammo decrement behavior consistent between both decompiled functions: subtract a computed cost from ip->xD4C and clamp negative values back to zero.
Added only the narrow include needed for it_80298DEC instead of leaving an implicit or unknown external dependency.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with concrete signatures in the same PR as the decompilation, improving future call-site type propagation.
decomp_lessons: When a function indexes into item attributes by a bounded charge level, padding may hide a real table; here a 0x28-byte pad became float xC[10].
Matched code may require retaining explicit PAD_STACK calls; this PR used PAD_STACK(16) in it_80291F14 and PAD_STACK(8) in it_80291FA8.
For item resource counters such as ip->xD4C, check for saturating/clamping behavior after subtraction rather than assuming unsigned arithmetic or direct assignment.
Header cleanup is part of decompilation: replacing UNK signatures with exact parameter and return types helps downstream functions match and documents inferred behavior.
naming_conventions: Unidentified Super Scope functions kept address-based names such as it_80291F14 and it_80291FA8.
Item object locals followed existing conventions: Item_GObj* gobj and Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj).
Attribute access followed existing convention: itSScopeAttributes* attrs = GET_ATTRS(ip).
The newly exposed attribute member uses offset-style provisional naming, xC, matching the struct-offset naming style already present with x4, x8, and x34.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used an explicit switch over charge_level with cases 0 through 9 rather than a simpler direct array access, likely to match original control flow.
Cast float table entries from attrs->xC[...] to int before subtracting from ip->xD4C.
Inserted PAD_STACK(16) and PAD_STACK(8) to satisfy stack-shape requirements for exact matching.
Verification in the PR body states all functions were 100% matched via objdiff and main.dol SHA1 was OK.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename itSScopeAttributes::xC to a semantic Super Scope ammo or charge cost table name.
Search for other uses of ip->xD4C in item code to confirm its Super Scope ammo/energy semantics.
Inspect it_80291D38 and it_80291DAC for related charge-level cost logic and possible future naming.
Search for it_80298DEC in itsscopebeam code to understand beam spawn parameters and owner handling.
